content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4

## Summary

Reviewed the new success-reconciliation flow and its supporting lineage/journal changes end-to-end. The close/sync/update ordering is sound and the new tests pass, but I found 2 correctness/robustness gaps: reconciliation-only retries still depend on worktree/branch recovery before the fast-path runs, and the handler reconstructs `run_id` from the latest project journal entry instead of persisting the task’s actual workflow run.

## Amendments

1. Fix the retry entry path for reconciliation-only failures. In src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:649-696 and src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1412-1446, the new `is_reconciliation_retry` guard runs only after `DaemonTaskService::claim_task(...)`. For the new reconciliation failure classes returned at src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1799-1828, `claim_task` still treats the retry as a generic failed retry (src/contexts/automation_runtime/task_service.rs:147-168) and `LeaseService::acquire` will create a worktree and attempt `try_resume_from_remote` before the reconciliation fast-path is reached (src/contexts/automation_runtime/lease_service.rs:210-263). As a result, a retry whose only remaining work is `br close/sync` plus milestone bookkeeping can still fail with unrelated worktree/network/auth errors before it ever reaches `try_reconcile_success`. The completed-run retry path needs to bypass or special-case worktree resume/acquire for the reconciliation-specific failure classes.
   Rationale: This breaks the intended idempotent recovery path: metadata-only reconciliation retries can be blocked by missing preserved branches or transient Git failures even though no code rerun is required.
2. Persist and use the task’s actual workflow `run_id` instead of reconstructing it from the latest project journal entry. In src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:1658-1779, `try_reconcile_success` finds the last `RunStarted`/`RunCompleted` in the project journal and assumes that run belongs to the failed daemon task. But `DaemonTask` does not durably store the workflow run identifier at all (src/contexts/automation_runtime/model.rs:49-87 only keeps `project_id` and `requirements_run_id`). If the project is rerun or manually resumed between the original reconciliation failure and a later `retry_task`, this code will reconcile against the newest run in the project journal rather than the run that actually belongs to the failed task, which can write the wrong completion timestamp / lineage repair or attach `task_id` to the wrong run. Store the workflow run_id on the task when dispatch starts/completes and read that here instead of guessing from 'latest RunStarted'.
   Rationale: Without a task-scoped run link, reconciliation retries become ambiguous once a project has more than one recorded run, and the current logic can misattribute milestone history.
